generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving identifier issues or invalid expressions within AGREE contracts.
Prioritize fixing unresolved references or type mismatches before modifying the underlying AADL architecture.
If an identifier is not visible in AGREE, remove the associated clause rather than attempting to introduce it through architecture changes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Ensure helper declarations have types consistent with their usage in expressions.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to directly use AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) as variables in AGREE expressions without explicit model exposure.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL scope rules for annex placement
